question.label: Unwritten rules
question.name: When you say 'unwritten rules,' what are you having to decode in meetings?
question.description: Drills into the specific norms or expectations she feels she is inferring so the manager can remove ambiguity.

answer: I can draft plans, though I am not always sure when I should decide versus escalate.

assessment.note: She names uncertainty about when to decide versus escalate, which signals mild strain from role ambiguity; also reveals a strong clarity gap worth probing next.

new_queue.label: Decide vs escalate
new_queue.name: On decide versus escalate, what cases feel least clear right now?

new_queue.label: Meeting uncertainty
new_queue.name: Which cross-functional meetings still feel hardest to step into?

new_queue.label: Alignment friction
new_queue.name: What part of the alignment work is taking more effort than it should?

new_queue.label: Manager boundaries
new_queue.name: What boundaries from me would help most right now?

history.answer_excerpt: I am settling in, but I still feel like I am learning the unwritten rules in every meeting.
I can draft plans, though I am not always sure when I should decide versus escalate.
I am settling in, but I still feel like I am learning the unwritten rules in every meeting.
